name: Bleeding Lance
description: The Bleeding Lance, also known as the Spear of Destiny, Holy Lance, or Lance of Longinus, is a legendary relic in Christian tradition. It is the lance believed to have pierced the side of Jesus Christ during his crucifixion, as described in the Gospel of John (John 19:34). The lance is said to possess immense power, often associated with destiny and the ability to control the course of history. Throughout history, numerous individuals and empires have sought to possess the lance, believing it would grant them invincibility and power over their enemies. Its ownership has been attributed to figures like Constantine the Great, Charlemagne, and Adolf Hitler, each believing in its power to shape their destinies. The lance is often depicted as a Roman spear, but its appearance and materials vary in different accounts. The blood of Christ that flowed from the wound is believed to have imbued the lance with sacred power. Some legends suggest that the lance grants invincibility as long as it is held, but brings ruin upon those who lose it. The spear's significance lies in its direct connection to the Passion of Christ, making it a powerful symbol of sacrifice, redemption, and divine power. The lance has been a subject of intense fascination and speculation for centuries, appearing in countless works of art, literature, and film.
subtitle: The Spear that Pierced Christ's Side
origin: Used to pierce Jesus Christ's side during the crucifixion.

keyMyths.title: The Piercing of Christ
keyMyths.description: The Roman soldier Longinus pierced Jesus' side with the lance to confirm his death. Blood and water flowed from the wound, solidifying the lance's sacred status.

keyMyths.title: Power and Possession
keyMyths.description: Legends claim that whoever possesses the lance controls destiny and is invincible. Many historical figures sought to obtain it for this perceived power.

sources.author: Gospel of John
sources.description: The primary biblical account of the lance piercing Jesus' side.
sources.text: The primary biblical account of the lance piercing Jesus' side.

sources.author: Trevor Ravenscroft
sources.description: A popular book exploring the lance's historical and mystical significance.
sources.text: A popular book exploring the lance's historical and mystical significance.
